testcase.name: test_send_dict

failure.message: requests.exceptions.HTTPError: 405 Client Error: Not Allowed for url: http://example.org/inbox

testcase.failure:
self = <test_sender.TestSender testMethod=test_send_dict> mock_post = <MagicMock name='post' id='4365910768'>
@patch('requests.post') def test_send_dict(self, mock_post): data = None with open("tests/notification.json", "r") as f: data = json.loads(f.read())
> Sender().send(self.INBOX, data)
tests/test_sender.py:31: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ ldnlib/sender.py:46: in send self.__post_message(inbox, json.dumps(data), self.JSON_LD, ldnlib/sender.py:39: in __post_message r.raise_for_status() _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <Response [405]>
def raise_for_status(self): """Raises :class:`HTTPError`, if one occurred."""
http_error_msg = "" if isinstance(self.reason, bytes): # We attempt to decode utf-8 first because some servers # choose to localize their reason strings. If the string # isn't utf-8, we fall back to iso-8859-1 for all other # encodings. (See PR #3538) try: reason = self.reason.decode("utf-8") except UnicodeDecodeError: reason = self.reason.decode("iso-8859-1") else: reason = self.reason
if 400 <= self.status_code < 500: http_error_msg = ( f"{self.status_code} Client Error: {reason} for url: {self.url}" )
elif 500 <= self.status_code < 600: http_error_msg = ( f"{self.status_code} Server Error: {reason} for url: {self.url}" )
if http_error_msg: > raise HTTPError(http_error_msg, response=self) E requests.exceptions.HTTPError: 405 Client Error: Not Allowed for url: http://example.org/inbox
.venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/requests/models.py:1026: HTTPError

testcase.name: test_send_graph
testcase.failure:
self = <test_sender.TestSender testMethod=test_send_graph> mock_post = <MagicMock name='post' id='4365911440'>
@patch('requests.post') def test_send_graph(self, mock_post): data = Graph().parse("tests/notification.nt", format="ntriples")
> Sender().send(self.INBOX, data)
tests/test_sender.py:51: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ ldnlib/sender.py:51: in send ct = self.__accept_post_options(inbox, **kwargs) or self.JSON_LD ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <ldnlib.sender.Sender object at 0x1043196d0> inbox = 'http://example.org/inbox', kwargs = {} future = <Future at 0x1043bf250 state=finished returned Response> r = <Response [405]>
def __accept_post_options(self, inbox, **kwargs): future = self.session.options(inbox, **kwargs) # Use FuturesSession r = future.result() # Get the response > if r.status_code == self.session.codes.ok and 'accept-post' in r.headers: ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E AttributeError: 'FuturesSession' object has no attribute 'codes'
ldnlib/sender.py:22: AttributeError

testcase.name: test_send_list

failure.message: requests.exceptions.HTTPError: 405 Client Error: Not Allowed for url: http://example.org/inbox

testcase.failure:
self = <test_sender.TestSender testMethod=test_send_list> mock_post = <MagicMock name='post' id='4365913456'>
@patch('requests.post') def test_send_list(self, mock_post): data = None with open("tests/notification.json", "r") as f: data = json.loads("[" + f.read() + "]")
> Sender().send(self.INBOX, data)
tests/test_sender.py:42: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ ldnlib/sender.py:46: in send self.__post_message(inbox, json.dumps(data), self.JSON_LD, ldnlib/sender.py:39: in __post_message r.raise_for_status() _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <Response [405]>
def raise_for_status(self): """Raises :class:`HTTPError`, if one occurred."""
http_error_msg = "" if isinstance(self.reason, bytes): # We attempt to decode utf-8 first because some servers # choose to localize their reason strings. If the string # isn't utf-8, we fall back to iso-8859-1 for all other # encodings. (See PR #3538) try: reason = self.reason.decode("utf-8") except UnicodeDecodeError: reason = self.reason.decode("iso-8859-1") else: reason = self.reason
if 400 <= self.status_code < 500: http_error_msg = ( f"{self.status_code} Client Error: {reason} for url: {self.url}" )
elif 500 <= self.status_code < 600: http_error_msg = ( f"{self.status_code} Server Error: {reason} for url: {self.url}" )
if http_error_msg: > raise HTTPError(http_error_msg, response=self) E requests.exceptions.HTTPError: 405 Client Error: Not Allowed for url: http://example.org/inbox
.venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/requests/models.py:1026: HTTPError

self = <Response [405]>
def raise_for_status(self): """Raises :class:`HTTPError`, if one occurred."""
http_error_msg = "" if isinstance(self.reason, bytes): # We attempt to decode utf-8 first because some servers # choose to localize their reason strings. If the string # isn't utf-8, we fall back to iso-8859-1 for all other # encodings. (See PR #3538) try: reason = self.reason.decode("utf-8") except UnicodeDecodeError: reason = self.reason.decode("iso-8859-1") else: reason = self.reason
if 400 <= self.status_code < 500: http_error_msg = ( f"{self.status_code} Client Error: {reason} for url: {self.url}" )
elif 500 <= self.status_code < 600: http_error_msg = ( f"{self.status_code} Server Error: {reason} for url: {self.url}" )
if http_error_msg: > raise HTTPError(http_error_msg, response=self) E requests.exceptions.HTTPError: 405 Client Error: Not Allowed for url: http://example.org/inbox
.venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/requests/models.py:1026: HTTPError
